## Structure

UDP C→S 0x03/0x07/0x03 — **Multipart with discriminator 0x03**.
1801-byte reassembled body / 2 fragments. Sample's ASCII
content "Important N..." matches the same Mail/note channel
as disc-1 and disc-2.

Same family as the S→C `udp_s2c_03_07_03` (which carries
"MailControl" payload). The C→S disc-1, disc-2, disc-3 all
appear to be variants of mail/note submission with different
sub-types.

```
[0..5]   multipart_header (per multipart_framing.md)
[6..]    [0x03 discriminator][total_size LE32][payload "Important N..."]
```

## Variants

Single 1801B reassembled retail sample.

## Observed contexts

CREATION_LEVELING_LONG only.

## Open questions

- C→S disc-1 / disc-2 / disc-3 all carrying "Important N..."
  in the same capture suggests these are 3 different
  send-attempts or different recipient addresses for the same
  note content.
- The S→C side has disc-2 (CharsysInfo, dead code), disc-3,
  and disc-4 (both MailControl). Discriminator semantics
  diverge between C→S and S→C directions.

## Server-side handler

Multipart reassembly catches but no consumer. **Low priority**
parity gap (1 retail sample).
